summary: PIN login rework verified end-to-end. Backend smoke tests (7/7 passed) confirm POST /api/auth/admin-pin-login accepts both email and phone identifiers with temp PIN 139848 returning session_token + pin_must_change=true, rejects wrong PIN with 401, and /api/auth/pin-change successfully rotates the PIN, invalidates the old one, and flips pin_must_change to false as reflected by GET /api/auth/me. Trivial PINs like 111111 are blocked server-side (400/422). Frontend Playwright tests confirm: landing-screen still branded 'S.K. Sharma & Co.', both admin-pin-login-button and employee-pin-login-button navigate to their respective screens with NO Expo 'Unmatched Route' 404, all required testIDs are present on the new screens (admin-identifier-input, admin-pin-input, admin-pin-submit, pin-company-code-input, pin-employee-code-input, pin-pin-input, pin-submit, pin-change-current/new/confirm/submit/error), full happy path (admin login with email 139848 -> /pin-change -> set new PIN 452801 -> /(tabs) dashboard 'Hello, S.K. Super Admin') works, client-side validation on /pin-change catches mismatched confirm, trivial-all-same-digit, and same-as-current PINs, employee /pin-login shows 'Invalid credentials' error banner (pin-error) for bogus company+employee code, and both cross-links ('Employee sign in instead' / 'Admin sign in instead') navigate correctly. Employee happy path not tested — no employee account exists (expected per request). Two low-priority React Native web console warnings observed (deprecated shadow* props and props.pointerEvents) — informational only, not blocking. Super-admin DB state has been reset to temp PIN 139848 + pin_must_change=true for downstream test agents.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' — emitted from src/theme shadow.card/shadow.cta objects. Non-blocking on native but noisy on web. Consider migrating to boxShadow-compatible values or platform-guarded shadows.
Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — likely from an internal RN Web component (Pressable/View). Consider auditing any pointerEvents={...} usage in the tree.

action_items: None blocking. Optional: address the two deprecated-prop console warnings (shadow*, pointerEvents) in src/theme.ts and any Pressable/View using pointerEvents prop to keep the web preview console clean.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend admin-pin-login.tsx, pin-login.tsx, and pin-change.tsx correctly guard against already-authenticated users via <Redirect> and never render on top of an active session — good UX and prevents session-token overwrite.
pin-change.tsx enforces both client- and server-side rules (length, all-same-digit, trivial list, same-as-current); redundant but defensive — acceptable.
AuthContext.refresh() is called after saveToken so the guard on / and /(tabs) sees the fresh user object with pin_must_change flag — flow is correct.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Super-admin PIN is reset in-place by the test fixture (bcrypt hash of 139848 + pin_must_change=true) before and after the pytest module, and manually re-reset after the frontend Playwright run so subsequent iterations can re-use temp PIN 139848.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Super-admin email=sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com / phone=+919680273960 / temp PIN=139848 (pin_must_change=true) — state has been reset. To re-run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_pin_auth_smoke.py -v`. If you need a fresh temp PIN in the DB run: `python -c "import asyncio,bcrypt; from motor.motor_asyncio import AsyncIOMotorClient; ...update pin_hash=bcrypt('139848')`. Employee PIN-login happy path is NOT covered — needs a seeded employee record with company_code + employee_code + pin_hash + role='employee'. Two RN web deprecation warnings exist but are informational.
rca_of_issue: Original bug ('Facing Error While Login Admin or User') was caused by /admin-pin-login and /pin-login routes being unregistered Expo Router stubs — clicking the landing buttons produced Expo's 'Unmatched Route' 404 screen. Main agent added three new screens (admin-pin-login.tsx, pin-login.tsx, pin-change.tsx) that call the corresponding /api/auth/* endpoints and integrate with AuthContext. Verified fix: from the landing screen, admin-pin-login-button now opens a fully functional PIN entry form, submits credentials, and forwards to /pin-change (when pin_must_change=true) or /(tabs) otherwise. No further code changes needed.
